recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — 4K phone filming a larger flat-panel monitor (not the small RCA from V23, and not the Insignia from V22). Camera positioned at an angle from the right side, closer than V23 but still showing window/room context. Pink/red foam block, Penrose drain, perforated board with moderate suture debris. White suture used. Window with blinds visible at right — same room as V23's RCA lab but appears to be a different/larger monitor. Resolution 3840x2160 at 24fps. Better framing than V23 but still at an angle.

frame_analyses.description: Drain visible on foam block. No instruments. Moderate debris on perforated board. Green/yellow glow on drain opening from monitor lighting. Clean starting position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No instruments yet. TIMING NOT STARTED.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments entering from right. White suture visible extending from instrument to drain area. Both instruments arriving at drain. TIMING STARTS ~5s.
Needle driving through drain. Both instruments at drain. White suture extending downward. Active needle pass.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle pass in progress. Efficient movement.

frame_analyses.description: Suture placed through both marks. Both suture ends visible from drain. Instruments at drain repositioning. White suture extending downward.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement completing ~24s. Fast placement — approximately 18s from first instrument.

frame_analyses.description: First throw beginning. Both instruments at drain with suture. Drain slightly lifted during manipulation. White suture visible extending downward. Instruments positioning for wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw starting ~28s. Suture placement complete ~26s (18-20s placement).

frame_analyses.description: First throw wrapping. Both instruments above drain. Suture lines extending from drain upward to instruments. Wrapping for surgeon's knot.
Second throw in progress. Knot visible at drain center with suture crossing. One instrument from upper left. Suture wraps visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw completed ~50s (~22s duration). Second throw underway.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw in progress. Both instruments at drain. Suture wrapping visible. Instrument from upper left with suture line going up. Knot building.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw completed ~64s (~14s). Third throw underway.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw completing. Knot visible at drain center — well-formed, drain compressed. Instrument from upper area with suture line. Suture tails extending. Knot appears secured.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw completing ~80-82s (~16s). Transitioning to cutting. Total throws: ~54s.

frame_analyses.description: CUTTING PHASE. Scissors clearly visible from upper left crossing over drain to cut suture tail. Other instrument from right stabilizing. Knot at center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting starts ~82-85s. Scissors in position for first cut.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting continues. One instrument from right at drain. Knot at center. One tail appears shorter — first cut may be done. Working on second tail.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 14+ seconds into cutting. Progress on first tail.

frame_analyses.description: Near end of cutting. One instrument at drain. Knot visible. Suture tail extending up. Drain intact.
Final cuts. One instrument still at drain from right. Knot visible at center with wrapping visible. Short tails. Drain compressed by knot — well-closed appearance.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting completing ~120-122s. Total cutting: ~38-40 seconds. MAJOR IMPROVEMENT from V21-V23's 57-65s.

frame_analyses.description: TASK COMPLETE. Instruments withdrawn. Knot visible at drain center — dark crossing pattern with short stubs. Drain appears well-closed with compression around knot. Drain centered on block, not avulsed. Short suture tails. Clean completion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: SECOND BEST TIME IN THE ENTIRE 24-VIDEO SERIES. Only V14 (107s) was faster. Completion at ~122s. Cutting at ~38-40s is a BREAKTHROUGH — 30% improvement from V22's 57s.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 26-50s (24s). Surgeon's knot assumed. Confidence: low — monitor angle, white suture.
Second throw 50-64s (14s). FASTEST second throw in recent series. Confidence: low.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Marks not visible from camera angle/distance. Default 2mm estimate. Suture appears through correct area.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 126s final frame, drain appears well-closed with knot centered. Drain compressed (hourglass shape) suggesting good tension. Short suture tails visible. No gap detected from monitor view. Drain not avulsed. Confidence: low-moderate.

technique_summary: V24 at 117 seconds is the SECOND BEST TIME IN THE ENTIRE 24-VIDEO SERIES — only 10 seconds behind V14's all-time best (107s). This represents a MASSIVE breakthrough from the V22-V23 plateau of ~153s. EVERY PHASE improved: placement 18s (series best), throws 56s total (best since V14), and critically CUTTING at 40s — a 30% improvement from V22's 57s and the fastest cutting since V14 (29s). The cutting breakthrough is the most significant development in the training series since V14. The trainee has clearly been working on cutting skills based on the consistent feedback from V15-V23. The overall trajectory from V20's 218s crash to V24's 117s represents a 46% improvement in just 4 videos. This performance validates the training methodology and suggests the trainee is approaching FLS examination readiness.
improvement_suggestions: Cutting at 40s is a BREAKTHROUGH — down from 57-65s plateau. Continue the cutting drills that led to this improvement.
Still 11 seconds behind V14's 29s cutting — further improvement possible with continued dedicated cutting practice.
First throw at 24s is the longest single phase — tighter transition from placement to throw could save 3-5s.
Recording quality still needs improvement — angle shot of monitor misses detail. Direct camera would provide 0.60+ confidence.
Confirm which attempt number this was in the session — if early, this is extraordinary. If late, even more impressive given fatigue resistance.
TARGET: Sub-107s (beat V14) is now realistically achievable. Cutting to 29s + current throw pace = ~105s.
strengths: SECOND BEST TIME EVER: 117 seconds — only V14 (107s) was faster
CUTTING BREAKTHROUGH: 40 seconds — 30% improvement from V22-V23 plateau (57-58s)
Throws at 56 seconds total — best since V14 (48s). All three throws efficient.
Placement at 18 seconds — fastest in recent series
Short, well-trimmed tails in final frame
Drain appears well-closed with good compression
Drain not avulsed — 24 consecutive videos without avulsion
4-video trajectory: V21 170s → V22 152s → V23 155s → V24 117s — exponential improvement curve
Validates the training feedback loop — cutting drills specifically addressed the identified bottleneck

phase_comparison.placement_18s: Series best recent. V14: 22s. V22: 20s. V23: 24s. FASTEST.
phase_comparison.throws_56s: Best since V14 (48s). V22: 70s. V23: 66s. 20% faster than V22.
phase_comparison.cutting_40s: BREAKTHROUGH. V14 best: 29s. V22: 57s. V23: 58s. 30% improvement from plateau.

key_observations: 117s / 477 FLS — SECOND BEST EVER. Only 10s behind V14 (107s/487).
CUTTING BREAKTHROUGH: 40s vs 57-65s plateau. This is the single most important improvement in the series since V14.
All phases improved simultaneously — placement, throws, AND cutting.
4-video improvement: 170→152→155→117. The V22-V23 plateau was a consolidation phase before breakthrough.
41% improvement from baseline (198s→117s) — approaching examination readiness territory.
101 seconds faster than V20 crash (218s). Complete recovery + significant new territory.
Throws at 56s total — consistent improvement trend: V21 74s → V22 70s → V23 66s → V24 56s.
Different monitor from V23 (larger flat panel vs small RCA) but same lab location.
Heavy debris still present — mid-session attempt, yet achieved near-peak performance. Excellent fatigue management.
Sub-120s performance suggests FLS examination passing score territory (varies by program, typically 400-470 FLS).
24th video: the training arc — baseline 198s → crash 218s → recovery 152s → breakthrough 117s — is textbook skill acquisition with plateau-breakthrough pattern.
